name: above
title: above
summary: Network security sniffer for finding vulnerabilities in the network
description:
Network security sniffer for finding vulnerabilities in the network
This package contains a network security sniffer for finding vulnerabilities
in the network, designed for pentesters and security professionals.
.
It is based entirely on network traffic analysis, so it does not make any
noise on the air. Above allows pentesters to automate the process of finding
vulnerabilities in network hardware. Discovery protocols, dynamic routing,
FHRP, STP, LLMNR/NBT-NS, etc.
.
The tool can also both listen to traffic on the interface and analyze already
existing pcap files.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use above because Network security sniffer for finding vulnerabilities in the network.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use above as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `above [options] [args]` as documented upstream for above.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair above with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `above --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
